name: Forte do Moinho Branco from Alhandra — circular route
name_original: Forte do Moinho Branco a partir de Alhandra — percurso circular
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Alhandra are primarily situated along the Tagus River and within its estuary region. The landscape is characterized by flat riverine paths, wetlands, and agricultural lands, offering open views of the Tagus Estuary. While generally low in elevation, some routes may include gentle inclines to viewpoints overlooking the river and surrounding floodplains. The area provides accessible terrain suitable for easy walks. Best easy hiking trails around Alhandra The most popular easy hiking route is Tagus Estuary loop from Póvoa, a 3.1 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This route offers views across the Tagus Estuary. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pathway Through the Reeds – Tagus Estuary loop from Alverca, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail navigates through wetland environments near the Tagus River. Local hikers also love the Viewpoint Over the Lagoon loop from Centro de Interpretação do EVOA, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Hiking around Alhandra is defined by the Tagus River pedestrian paths, the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy trails in this guide. The routes in Alhandra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Alhandra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Alhandra?
A: This guide features 7 easy hiking trails around Alhandra. These routes primarily explore the flat riverine paths and wetlands along the Tagus River and within the Tagus Estuary region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes around Alhandra?
A: The easy trails in Alhandra are characterized by flat riverine paths, wetlands, and agricultural lands. You'll enjoy open views of the Tagus Estuary. While generally low in elevation, some routes may include gentle inclines leading to viewpoints overlooking the river and surrounding floodplains.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in Alhandra?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails around Alhandra are circular. For example, you can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus Estuary loop from Póvoa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Over the Lagoon loop from Centro de Interpretação do EVOA&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve.

Q: Are the easy trails in Alhandra suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the flat and accessible nature of many easy trails, particularly those along the Tagus River pedestrian path, makes them suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pathway Through the Reeds – Tagus Estuary loop from Alverca&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for exploring wetland environments with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails in Alhandra?
A: Many of the easy trails in Alhandra, especially the open riverine paths and those through agricultural lands, are generally dog-friendly. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and respect local regulations, particularly when passing through nature reserves or near wildlife habitats.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see on these easy hikes?
A: Along the easy hiking trails, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7293438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus Estuary&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major natural attraction. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cais da Jorna and Fishermen's Monument&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Constantino Palha Municipal Garden&lt;/a&gt;. The Miradouro de Alhandra offers panoramic views of the Tagus River and surrounding areas.

Q: How long do easy hikes in Alhandra typically take?
A: Easy hikes in Alhandra typically range from 1 to 2 hours to complete. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus Estuary loop from Póvoa&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour 14 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32160632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus Estuary loop from Scania Portugal&lt;/a&gt; is around 1 hour 50 minutes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alhandra?
A: The best time to hike in Alhandra is generally during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though still suitable for hiking on many days.

Q: Are there options for parking or public transport access to these easy trails?
A: Yes, Alhandra and nearby towns like Vila Franca de Xira offer various parking options, especially near the riverfront and town centers, which often serve as starting points for trails. Public transport, including train services along the Tagus River, can also provide access to different sections of the pedestrian paths and trailheads.

Q: What makes the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve a special place for hiking?
A: The Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, located near Alhandra, is one of Portugal's largest wetlands, renowned for its exceptional biodiversity. Hiking here offers opportunities to observe a wide variety of species, including migratory birds and marine life, within diverse landscapes of estuarine waters, sandbanks, and agricultural lands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Over the Lagoon loop from Centro de Interpretação do EVOA&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience this area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in Alhandra?
A: The easy trails in Alhandra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the peaceful river views, the flat and accessible terrain, and the opportunities for birdwatching within the Tagus Estuary region.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, some easy trails provide excellent panoramic views. While many routes are flat, those that lead to or pass by viewpoints like the Miradouro de Alhandra offer breathtaking vistas of the Tagus River, Vila Franca de Xira, and the vast Lezírias (floodplains). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19405791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Mill loop from Cardosas&lt;/a&gt;, though it has more elevation gain, also offers rewarding views.
tips: Founded in 1558 by Cardinal D. Henrique, the main church was a majestic temple for the time. After a fire, it was completely rebuilt with simple, unadorned architecture. Its interior, however, houses a valuable collection of sacred art from the 17th and 18th centuries, including pieces from the original church and other churches in Lisbon.

Designed by Lieutenant Colonel Joaquim da Costa Cascaes and approved in 1874, the monument commemorates the Anglo-Portuguese victory over Napoleon and the construction of the Lines of Torres Vedras. Completed in 1883, it features a statue of Hercules, the work of the sculptor Simões de Almeida, with classical attributes.

The Lines of Torres Vedras are 152 defensive fortifications, built between 1809 and 1812 near Lisbon, which prevented the French advance in the Peninsular War.
The Forte do Moinho Branco (or Sinais) is one of these buildings.
